---
title: "geom_marquee_colored"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L1761)

# `geom_marquee_colored()`

Create a Color-Matched Label Layer

## Usage

```r
geom_marquee_colored(data, mapping, ..., bold = FALSE, italic = FALSE)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `data` | Data frame containing the data. |
| `mapping` | Aesthetic mapping created by `aes()`. Must include `x`, `y`, `label`, and `color` (the color to use for the text). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |
| `bold` | Make text bold. Default: FALSE. |
| `italic` | Make text italic. Default: FALSE. |

## Value

A ggplot2 layer object.

## Description

A convenience function that adds colored text labels using marquee.
Pre-formats labels with marquee color syntax before plotting.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(marquee)
df <- data.frame(
    x = c("A", "B", "C"),
    y = c(10, -5, 8),
    text_color = c("#006400", "#8B0000", "#006400"),
    label = c("+10", "-5", "+8")
)
ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
    geom_col(aes(fill = text_color)) +
    geom_marquee_colored(
        data = df,
        mapping = aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = text_color),
        vjust = -0.5
    )

## End(Not run)
```
